data.narrative:
Neros is a Series B–stage defense and security technology company founded by Soren Monroe-Anderson and Olaf Hichwa, having raised $110M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage.

Two primary valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy, applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $209M. Peer-median benchmarking across 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables—including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns—establishes a median valuation of $1.2B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.5B.

The triangulated analysis across both methods produces a valuation range of $178M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $704M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty: stage-progression conservatively anchors to capitalization history, while peer multiples suggest considerably higher valuations typical of well-capitalized defense-tech entrants. Key sensitivities include Neros's revenue traction relative to the peer set, differentiation within an increasingly competitive frontier-defense landscape, and regulatory and procurement dynamics affecting comparables. Resolution of these factors will be critical to narrowing the range.
